message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="Design decisions on all 4 contrast findings, token change approved">
Heydon, excellent audit. Clear, actionable, and thorough. Here are my design decisions based on your findings:

**1. Muted text -- I accept your recommendation with a two-tier approach.**

You're right that gray-500 at 4.6:1 is too tight for body text at small sizes. Here's what I want:

- `--color-fg-muted`: Change to gray-600 (oklch 0.45) -- ~7.4:1. This is our safe "secondary text" color. Used for: muted labels, secondary descriptions, footer text, timestamps, the "Already have an account?" text below auth forms. Any text that a user needs to read.
- `--color-fg-subtle`: Keep at gray-400 (oklch 0.71) -- restricted to placeholders and decorative text ONLY. Never for content the user needs to read. The visible `<label>` requirement ensures placeholders are supplementary.

This means the auth form footer text ("Already have an account? Sign in") and field hints ("Must be at least 8 characters") will use `--color-fg-muted` (gray-600) instead of the lighter gray-500 I originally specified. Slightly less "airy" but safely readable. The right tradeoff.

**2. Gray-400 restricted to placeholders -- agreed.**
Already the design intent. Gray-400 (`--color-fg-subtle`) is for placeholder text and decorative elements only. Every field has a visible label. No gray-400 for body text ever.

**3. Success green -- icons and indicators only, not text.**
Agreed. I'll never spec success green for body text. It's for:
- The checkbox fill/checkmark on completed todos
- A subtle background tint (`--color-success-light`) on success banners
- Decorative indicators paired with gray-800 text

If we need a "Todo completed" announcement, it uses gray-800 text, not green text. The green is visual seasoning, not the message carrier.

**4. Verify chromatic colors with rendered sRGB values.**
Good call. When Luca has the app running, we should render the primary blue, danger red, and success green in a browser and run them through a contrast checker with the actual computed hex values. I'll add this to our Definition of Done checklist for the first feature that uses these colors (registration).

**Updated token change needed:**
Lea -- please update `--color-fg-muted` to point to gray-600 instead of gray-500:
```
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white) */
```

This is a design token governance change. I'm approving it based on Heydon's accessibility audit. The comments should note the contrast ratio.

Heydon, thanks for catching this before we built UI on top of it. This is exactly why accessibility review happens at the foundation level, not after the fact.
</teammate-message>

content.text: This has already been implemented -- commit `28691db` ("Bump --color-fg-muted to gray-600 for WCAG AA contrast safety") already made this change. I verified it when I read `main.css` earlier: line 212 shows `--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-600)` with the ~7.4:1 comment.

input.content:
Steve, this is already done. Commit `28691db` bumped `--color-fg-muted` to gray-600. Line 212 of `main.css` reads:

```css
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white -- comfortable AA for all text sizes) */
```

All four decisions are in place:
1. `--color-fg-muted` at gray-600 (~7.4:1) -- committed
2. `--color-fg-subtle` at gray-400 -- placeholders only, with warning comment -- committed
3. Success green restricted to icons/indicators -- documented in the color comment block
4. Chromatic color verification -- agreed, add to DoD for first feature using them

No further action needed on this. The foundation is solid.
input.summary: Token change already committed, all 4 decisions in place
